target.name: Classiq

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $95M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Classiq is a Series C quantum computing software company that has raised $95M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 peers across quantum computing—three public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and eight private entities including IonQ, Quantinuum, Infleqtion, and others—though same-stage private comparables remain sparse in this nascent sector.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation technique, using the $95M total capital raised as a proxy for recent valuation baseline and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-growth stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $162M. Peer-median analysis was not pursued due to insufficient same-stage or narrow adjacent-stage quantum computing comparables; applying all-sector peers would introduce unacceptable methodological noise given the sector's heterogeneity.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $137M (low) to $186M (high), with a median point of $162M. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer comparables at identical stage and tier, limiting triangulation to a single method, and broader quantum computing sector maturation risk. The $95M capital base and derived $162M median reflect software-layer positioning within a hardware-dominated sector.
